calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Designating That Certain Real Property And Personal Property Belongs To The Justin Winfrey Revocable Trust
Off Calendar Jan-11-2022 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Trial
Off Calendar Jan-11-2022 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Nov-02-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Greer Smith, via ZOOM, for Jasmine Jones-Herring, Petitioner; Azita Rahim and John Rueppel, via ZOOM, for Ana Robledo, Objector; Leighton Burrey, via ZOOM, Guardian Ad Litem for Carter J. Winfrey.

The court states that the joint case management statement ("CMS") filed on Oct-22-2021 is similar to the one filed Aug-31-2021. Ms. Smith reports that the mediation has been continued to Nov-17-2021. The court asks if there is an update regarding the DNA test. Ms. Smith reports that she received the test from Mr. Burrey and he confirms sending the test to Ms. Smith and also reports that paternity will not be confirmed by the test. The court asks Mr. Burrey why he did not sign the joint CMS. Ms. Smith reports that Mr. Rueppel and Mr. Burrey believed that he did not need to sign the statement. The court advises the parties that if Mr. Burrey will be participating in the trial that he should sign both the CMS and joint trial plan. The court confirms that Mr. Burrey has not filed an opposition and advises him to file his own or joined with Ms. Robledo and notes that Mr. Burrey can file any objection up to the trial. The court then summarizes what will occur on Jan-11-2022 in Dept. 204 if this matter has not resolved, including sending the matter to the presiding Judge's Dept. for trial assignment.

The Case Management Hearing is taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Sep-14-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Rueppel and Azita Rahim, via ZOOM, for Ana Robledo, Special Administrator; Leighton Burrey, via ZOOM, Guardian Ad Litem; Greer Smith, via ZOOM, for Jasmine Jones-Herring, via ZOOM, Petitioner.

Mr. Rueppel begins by reporting that the Solano County Court has granted the demurrer without leave to amend and that mediation is possible. Ms. Smith reports that there has been an agreement to Betty Epstein serving as mediator but that she has yet to receive a reply from Mr. Rueppel. Mr. Rueppel advises that he needs to contact his client to confirm dates. The court then orders the parties to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed by no later than 10/22/2021. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner regarding the status of mediation by 10/27/2021. Ms. Smith is instructed to monitor the court's website for the order regarding Pro Bono Mediation and to serve said order on all parties who require notice. The court also instructs Mr. Rueppel to reply to Ms. Smith regarding his and his client's availability for mediation by noon on 9/17/2021.

The Case Management Hearing is taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Order Designating That Certain Real Property And Personal Property Belongs To The Justin Winfrey Revocable Trust
Off Calendar Jun-08-2021 Continued To Jan-11-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Adam Lazar, via ZOOM, counsel for Ana Robledo, Special Administrator; Robert Patterson, via ZOOM, counsel for Ana Robledo, Creditor; Greer Smith, via ZOOM, counsel for Jasmine Jones-Herring, Petitioner; James DuCharme, via ZOOM, counsel for Norma Banks, Trustee; Leighton Burrey, Guardian Ad Litem.

The court begins by advising counsel that it appears as though the issue of venue in this matter remains unsettled and whether it should move forward in light of the pending trial date currently set for October in a related matter in Solano County. Counsel provides oral argument to support their position including Mr. Lazar's report that the parties have discussed possibly requesting the court send this matter to pro bono mediation. The court advises counsel that it would only consider ordering this matter to the pro bono mediation once discovery is complete to avoid wasting resources.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Order Designating, etc., is set for trial in Department 204 on January 11, 2022, at 9:00 a.m. Case Management Conference dates are set for September 14, 2021 and November 2, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204. A copy of the order and notice of trial was mailed to Counsel.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Nov-04-2020 Advanced From Feb-16-21 To Dec-15-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Designating That Certain Real Property And Personal Property Belongs To The Justin Winfrey Revocable Trust
Off Calendar Jan-19-2021 Continued To Mar-09-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Greer Smith appeared by zoom for and with Jasmine Jones-Herring by zoom.
Garry Montinari (insurance defense Counsel for the representative of the Estate of Justin Winfrey) appeared by telephone.
James DuCharme appeared by zoom for Norma Banks.
Bob Patterson appeared by zoom for Ana Robledo.
John Rueppel appeared in court for Ana Robledo.

The Court stated that it can't get too far today because there are procedural issues that need to be addressed. Mr. Rueppel stated that he believes Mr. Patterson has petitioned for appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem ("GAL"), that they found an Attorney that is willing to serve as the GAL in the estate and that he did not provide service of the ex parte because he wasn't sure if it was a drop off, or if it required a hearing. the Court raised the issue of venue and noted that the Trustee will be living in Las Vegas. Ms. Smith stated that the Client's CPA resides here and is assisting with the day to day activities, and that all of the other cases are here. The Court stated that it doubts that the CPA will be showing the property, participating in the evictions and that it needs specific verified allegations to show that venue is appropriate in San Francisco. Mr. DuCharme asked if it is acceptable to the Court if the Trustee stipulates to this Court having jurisdiction and expressed concern about the Las Vegas Court having a different position. Discussion was held with regard to the legislative guidelines regarding venue. The Petition for Order Designating that Certain Real Property and Personal Property Belongs to the Justin Winfrey Revocable Trust is continued to March 9, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
